Squeeze & Exhaustion Risk Indicators

Squeeze & Exhaustion Risk Indicators

CoT tactical squeeze and exhaustion risk signals.

As of 2026-03-31

Gemini Summary

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given a Squeeze_z of -0.04 and an Exhaustion_z of 0.15, this setup aligns with Range-biased price paths and Normal volatility, where the dominant risk is Mean reversion, not Regime shift.
  • The signal currently occupies a Normal regime for both squeeze and exhaustion breadth (1).
  • Conviction Band: Medium; Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: No.
  • Signal Stability Assessment: Improving; Threshold Proximity: Moderate; Revision Sensitivity: Low.

Methodology Applied:

  • Z-scores below 0.5 define a Normal regime where tactical risks align with historical medians (1).
  • Exhaustion_z measures trend fatigue; values above 1.0 imply a high probability of trend reversal (1).
  • Squeeze_z measures forced covering risk; high breadth indicates vulnerability to flow-driven price spikes (1).
  • Dataset: CoT Tactical Breadth (Latest: 2026-03-31).

Key Dynamics:

  • Exhaustion breadth has stabilised following a peak of 0.94 on March 3 (1).
  • Squeeze risk has recovered from extreme lows (-0.98) to a neutral level (-0.04) (1).
  • There are no internal tensions as both metrics confirm a lack of extreme tactical stress.
  • Conditional Invalidation: Any component z-score crossing above the 1.0 threshold for High Risk (1).
  • The signal shows improving stability as it moves away from earlier "Moderate" risk levels.

Scenario Balance:

  • Base Case dominant: Current market trends persist without immediate flow-driven structural failure.
  • Upside secondary: Sustained low breadth signals clean positioning and potential for trend extensions.
  • Downside residual: Rapid rise in exhaustion breadth indicates widespread trend fatigue and reversal.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Tactical (weeks): The signal monitors short-term positioning fragility and trend maturity (1).
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Low; current "Normal" readings suggest no immediate tactical headwind for macro trends.

Macro Relevance:

  • This signal informs the sentiment and positioning dimension of the macro complex (1).
  • Economic mechanism: Identifies potential for non-fundamental volatility driven by forced liquidation or momentum decay.
  • Cycle position: Not determined.
  • Interacts with VIX and Credit Spreads to confirm systemic or tactical stress regimes (1).

Regime Context:

  • The Exhaustion regime transitioned from Moderate Risk back to Normal over the last three weeks (1).
  • Direction of change: Stabilising at neutral levels following a period of moderate trend fatigue.

Model Limitations:

  • Sector-agnostic approach ignores potential concentration risks in specific asset classes (1).
  • Reporting frequency for CoT data is inherently lagging.

Data & References:

  • Latest Data: 2026-03-31; influential datapoints: Squeeze_z (-0.04) and Exhaustion_z (0.15) (1).
  • Public datasets: VIX Index and MOVE Index for cross-confirmation of volatility regimes.
